MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for Keryl Harris (administrator).

Mrs. Navia stated that an accounting is ready, but the only issue is the estate's interests in several small properties in Texas, and that she plans on filing another accounting and ask for preliminary distribution. Mrs. Navia also stated that the timeline to close the estate depends on what happens in Texas and that they are hoping that the family purchases the decedent's interests. The court informed Mrs. Navia that she needs to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200. Status on close of estate is continued to October 21, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Magdaleno. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky appeared by zoom for special administrator.
Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for Keryl Harris (executor).

Mrs. Navia stated that she spoke to the attorney for Kenneth Salters, that the attorney is insisting that the estate pay for the certified letters and that she has to discuss this with him. Mrs. Navia also stated that one of the assets is a 20% interest in several properties that are in Louisiana, that she was told by Kim Hayes (Louisiana attorney) that they are in the process of selling the properties, that the other estate of Donald Jerryson holds a 20% interest and that it is difficult for her client to close this estate. In addition, Mrs. Navia stated that she hopes to have some movement in about four months from the attorney in Louisiana and requested a six-month continuance. Mr. Karlinsky stated that he has no objections to the petitioner's accounting, that he is aware this is somewhat complicated and that he has no problem with Mrs. Navia's client going full speed to resolve the Louisiana matter. The Petition for Approval of Fees of Special Administrator, etc., and the First Account and Report of Executor, etc. are continued to August 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Upon further consideration, there being no objection to the accounting, the Court waives defective notice and grants the accounting petition. The First Account and Report of Executor, etc. is under submission with the court's intent to grant. The Petition for Approval of Fees of Special Administrator, etc., is continued to August 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is also set for August 23, 2023.

Petition For Approval Of Fees Of Special Administrator And Attorney For Special Administrator
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Continued To Mar-03-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky appeared by zoom for Petitioner.
Adriennette Ciccone appeared by zoom specially appearing for Attorney Verleanna Green on behalf of Keryl Harris (Executor).

The Court asked Mr. Karlinsky if he appreciates the restrictions in Probate 10805 and CRC 7.704 which requires him to work with the other Attorney to split the statutory fees. Mr. Karlinsky stated that he does understand the requirement, and that he feels the realm of services he provided were in the form of extraordinary services as opposed to ordinary services. Mr. Karlinsky also stated that he wasn't sure how to get this issue before the Court, and that he brought this petition to make sure it was before the Court and that it did not get lost in the shuffle. Ms. Ciccone stated that they are still collecting assets, that they have received $230,000.00 from another out of State probate, and that the inventory and appraisal will be filed around the end of 2021 to add the additional assets. The Court explained that it is too soon for Mr. Karlinsky to request fees and informed him that it can deny the petition without prejudice, or continue it to a time when fees are being discussed. Mr. Karlinsky stated that he prefers the latter. Ms. Ciccone informed the Court that Ms. Green is out on leave and requested a further out continuance. Mr. Karlinsky made the suggestion to provide supplemental briefing on the issue of extraordinary fees. The Petition for Approval of Fees of Special Administrator and Attorney is continued to March 3, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The hearing on status on close of estate that is currently set for December 30, 2020 is also continued to March 3, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joscelyn Jones Torru for and with (petitioner) Keryl Harris, (witnesses) Melissa Wheeler and Genea Saulter.

Ms. Torru stated this petition is not contested and Ms. Scrivnor's competing petition has been withdrawn.

The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is Taken Under Submission. Status on Close of Estate is set for December 30, 2020.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Wu. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-03-2019 Continued To Oct-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky for Petitioner and for and with Mike Brumbaugh (Special Administrator).
Joceyln Jones-Torru for and with Keryl Harris (Objector).

The Court noted that a competing petition has been filed with a hearing date of October 2nd and that the purpose of today's hearing is to extend the special letters. Mr. Torru stated that she e-filed objections to the extension of special letters on Friday, that she has had communication with Mr. Karlinsky and that she was informed that Mr. Karlinsky's Client wants to proceed on the theory that her Client has forfieted her right to serve because of her late filing. The Court questions whether there is a need to extend the expiration of the special letters. Ms. Torru stated there is nothing that needs to be done, that no rent is being collected, that there are no bills, that no one is in the house and that there is no urgency. Mr. Karlinsky stated that there is some mistrust amongst the family, that his Client's desire is to have a professional, and that other than protection and preservation of the property, there is nothing that needs to be done. Mr. Karlinsky also stated that they have procured insurance, that he feels the status quo should be maintained and raised the question of liability of the Special Administrator if something were to happen between now and October 2nd. The Court noted that it does not see how someone with no authority could hold liability. Ms. Torru stated that the Neighbors have been and are watching out. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to October 2, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The request to extend special letters is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
